conclusion.initial_seed_optin_status: Tested after the first matrix: it was rejected because the x-block seed residual was slightly worse than the RHS norm and the run still stalled at 5.41e-6, so it remains opt-in and is not a default performance path.
conclusion.next_algorithmic_need: different global coupling strategy before or inside Krylov, rather than a larger timeout, full sparse materialization, default initial seed, scalar post-minres cleanup, or the current small post-coarse residual subspace.
conclusion.observed_blocker: x-block seeding improves the residual but all existing full sparse/direct/least-squares/Krylov rescue routes either time out, fail factorization, or stall near 5e-6 against a 2.5e-11 target.
conclusion.post_coarse1_optin_status: Tested as a stronger multidirectional matrix-free coarse hook. It accepted one 10-direction least-squares step and reduced the residual only from 5.413504e-6 to 5.401187e-6, so it remains opt-in and is not sufficient for closing scale-0.50 QI.
conclusion.post_minres4_optin_status: Tested as the first matrix-free correction hook. It accepted two steps and reduced the residual only from 5.413504e-6 to 5.409759e-6, so it remains opt-in and is not sufficient for closing scale-0.50 QI.

evidence_note: Scale-0.50 QI blocker matrix. These are diagnostic failed runs, not production promotion evidence. The matrix preserves solver-stage breadcrumbs without checking in copied VMEC/run directories.
